creator: rezny@freebsd.org
text:
Thank you for taking the effort to do an update. However, I have also been preparing an update to 0.16.0 which is almost ready. There is just one outstanding question to python team that I've been been trying to get answered to be sure gstreamer1 bits are correct. I am well aware of the patches that are no longer needed since I am the one who sent those upstream.

Updates should always be supplied as patches. Shar should never be used for a patch because I can't neither read it properly in bugzilla nor on my machine (I'm not about to execute a random shell script to extract a patch). I did take a glance at the shar in bugzilla, messy as that is, to see that the change from gstreamer 0.10 to 1.x is not handled in your patch.
